content_en:
It was a well-known fact that the prayers of Sa’d bin Abi Waqas < were like a sharp sword and were always answered.

listen and continued. Finally, Sa’d bin Abi Waqas 4 lost his temper, and said if did not control his tongue he would put a curse on him. Arrogantly the man told him that he was arrogating powers to himself and he asked him if he thought he was a Prophet 38. Listening to the man’s bitter words, he merely turned away. He went, made his ablutions for prayers and offered two supererogatory units of prayer. Then he entreated Allah Almighty:

“O Allah you know that this man is abusing men of purity, with whom You Yourself are Pleased because of their goodness. Surely You will not approve of these abuses. May this man be a lesson for such other men.”

He had barely done with his supplications when people saw that a raging she-camel had broken away from the rope which tethered her, and leaving her home had entered in the midst of a crowd of people. It seemed as if she was looking for a particular person. In seconds she had the head of a person in her bloodthirsty jaws and was shaking it violently from side to side. Finally his neck broke and in no time he had become the prey of death. People were stunned into silence. The dead man was none other than the one who had been abusing the noble Companions a few minutes earlier, and against whom Sa’d bin Abi Waqas -% had appealed to Allah 3%. He had indeed become a lesson for all people. There is no doubt that if any person abuses the loyal sacrificing Companion of the Prophet 38, Allah 3% will make them object lessons for others. He meets such a terrible fate that onlookers beg for the mercy of Ailah Almighty. Surely the love of the Companion is the precursor of the love for the Prophet 38, and the love of the Prophet 38 is the precursor and proof of the love for Allah Almighty Himself. Whoever resents and hates and nurtures enmity with these pure-hearted men is sure to become the prey of destruction and devastation in Eternity. Whosoever expresses love and affection for them will be twice blessed - now and in the Hereafter.

May Allah && fill our hearts with love for these celestial and sacred souls who thought it an honour to give up their lives at a mere signal from the Prophet 3; and on the Day of Judgement may he raise us among them.

The Prophet 38 said even this was a bit too much though it was all tight. He asked him to remember that it was better to leave one’s heirs in a good financial position; it was not good to leave them in a situation where they would have to around asking others for help. Allah will reward the person who spent anything at all in charity for Him.

The sickness of Sa’d bin Abi Waqas - was getting more and more complicated day by day. He loved Al-Madinah deeply and he wanted to die there. When the Prophet 38 saw his restlessness and desire to be in his favourite city, he put his blessed hand on his breast and supplicated to Allah Almighty thrice to cure him.

During this same illness, the Prophet 4 had predicted that Sa’d would not die until he had benefited one race of people and caused harm to another. This prediction turned out to be a hundred percent correct. The non-Arab people suffered badly by him and the Arabs gained a lot of advantages. After he recovered from his illness, he made many time married and had about thirty-four sons and daughters.

Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas -4 used to weep copiously out of fear of Allah 38. Whenever the Prophet % gave a sermon his eyes used to fill involuntarily with tears.

One day when the Prophet 3 was sitting in the company of his Companion, he looked up at them and said they were soon going to be joined by a Jannati.!

The entire Companion kept looking around expectantly to see whom this fortunate man could possibly be, about whom the Prophet 3% was giving this glad tiding. And soon Sa’d bin Abi Waqas appeared, and everyone started looking at him enviously and continued staring at him.

This was the man whose supplications were answered, pure of tongue, pure of heart and soul and a great warrior. In all the battles in which he took part with the Prophet 3%, at Badr, at Uhud and at many others he had proved innumerable times his daring, his bravery, his steadfastness and heroic qualities. These exploits fill golden chapters of our history.

‘Umar Farooq - was very impressed by the strength of his faith and the firm and truthfulness. He could never forget that incident which depicted the degree of his devotion to Islam. He had accepted Islam as a young boy; this disturbed and saddened his mother very deeply because he had given up the faith of his fathers and forefathers. She tried in many ways to bring him back into the fold of Kuffar. When nothing worked she used the last resort of mothers to emotionally blackmail her obedient and loving son. This is something, which makes their determination falter and their resolutions fail. She went on a hunger strike and declared she would not eat until her son came back to the religion of his forefathers. She vowed that rather die than see her son become a Muslim.

Sa’d bin Abi Waqas 8 was unnerved when he saw her stubbornness, but pure faith had taken strong roots in his heart, so his strong feet,

' A person who was destined to go to Jannah (Paradise).

firmly planted in Islam, did not totter. His mother was close to death by starvation and thirst. Displaying strong determination and courage he said to her:

“Dear mother if you had a hundred lives within your body, and each one of those hundred lives were to leave your body in front of my eyes, even then I would not recant and give up my faith in Islam. It is your wish whether you want to eat or not; as for myself, I will not leave my Prophet 38.”

Seeing that it was of no use and her son was stubborn as a rock, she finished her hunger strike. This resolution and determination of Sa’d bin Abi Waqas -# has been made immortal:

“But if they (both) strive with you to make you join in worship with Me others that of which you have no knowledge, then obey them not; but behave with them in the world kindly, and follow the path of him who turns to Me in repentance and in obedience. Then to Me will be your return, and I shall tell you what you used to do.” (31:15)

“Remember the importance of Qadisiyah, which is a historic place. If the battle is won then the whole of Iran will be at your feet. Qadisiyah is the central door to Iran. Do not be lost in arrogance because you are related to the Prophet 3%. Do not forget that one’s status and position in the eyes of Allah 38 depends on one’s obedience and loyalty to him. Allah Almighty is the Supporter and Sustainer of all and you are his slave. Keep

your eyes focused on the mission of the Prophet #8, which he bequeathed to us when he left this world.”

At the end of the letter he told him to keep me informed about all the latest developments. How would he enter the battlefield? Where would he camp? At what distance was the enemy at that time? In other words, he wanted detailed information about what was taking place every moment, as if he were able to see him.

Sa‘d bin Abi Waqas +, fulfilling his duties, kept the government at the centre informed regarding every detail to the extent that he kept Caliph ‘Umar Farooq -# informed in exhaustive detail about the welfare and activities of each Mujahid. He also wrote to him regarding the individual and collective assignments given to each Mujahid.
content_id:
Sudah menjadi fakta yang diketahui luas bahwa doa-doa Sa'd bin Abi Waqas bagaikan pedang tajam dan selalu dikabulkan.

mendengarkan dan terus melanjutkan. Akhirnya, Sa'd bin Abi Waqas kehilangan kesabaran, dan berkata jika orang itu tidak menahan lidahnya, ia akan mendoakan keburukan atasnya. Dengan sombong, orang itu mengatakan bahwa Sa'd mengklaim kekuasaan untuk dirinya sendiri dan bertanya apakah ia mengira dirinya seorang Nabi. Mendengar kata-kata pahit orang itu, Sa'd hanya berpaling. Ia pergi, mengambil wudhu dan menunaikan dua rakaat salah sunnah. Kemudian ia memohon kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa:

"Ya Allah, Engkau tahu bahwa orang ini mencaci maki orang-orang suci, yang Engkau sendiri ridha terhadap mereka karena kebaikan mereka. Tentu Engkau tidak akan merestui cacian-cacian ini. Semoga orang ini menjadi pelajaran bagi orang-orang sepertinya."

Ia baru saja selesai berdoa ketika orang-orang melihat seekor unta betina yang mengamuk telah melepaskan diri dari tali yang mengikatnya, dan meninggalkan tempatnya lalu masuk ke tengah kerumunan orang. Tampak seolah-olah unta itu sedang mencari seseorang tertentu. Dalam hitungan detik, kepala seseorang sudah berada di rahangnya yang haus darah dan ia mengguncangnya dengan keras dari sisi ke sisi. Akhirnya lehernya patah dan dalam sekejap ia telah menjadi mangsa kematian. Orang-orang terdiam membisu. Orang yang tewas itu tidak lain adalah orang yang beberapa menit sebelumnya mencaci maki para sahabat yang mulia, dan yang terhadapnya Sa'd bin Abi Waqas telah memohon kepada Allah. Orang itu memang telah menjadi pelajaran bagi semua orang. Tidak diragukan lagi bahwa jika seseorang mencaci maki para sahabat Nabi yang setia dan berkorban, Allah akan menjadikan mereka pelajaran bagi orang lain. Ia menemui nasib yang begitu mengerikan sehingga para saksi mata memohon rahmat Allah Yang Maha Kuasa. Sungguh, kecintaan terhadap para sahabat adalah pendahulu dari kecintaan kepada Nabi, dan kecintaan kepada Nabi adalah pendahulu dan bukti kecintaan kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa. Barangsiapa membenci dan memusuhi serta memelihara permusuhan dengan orang-orang yang berhati suci ini, pasti akan menjadi mangsa kehancuran dan kebinasaan di Hari Akhirat. Barangsiapa mengungkapkan cinta dan kasih sayang kepada mereka akan mendapat berkah ganda — di dunia dan di akhirat.

Semoga Allah memenuhi hati kita dengan cinta kepada jiwa-jiwa suci nan mulia ini yang menganggapnya sebagai kehormatan untuk mengorbankan nyawa mereka atas isyarat Nabi semata; dan pada Hari Kiamat semoga Allah membangkitkan kita bersama mereka.

Nabi bersabda bahwa itu pun sudah terlalu banyak meskipun tidak mengapa. Beliau memintanya untuk mengingat bahwa lebih baik meninggalkan ahli waris dalam kondisi keuangan yang baik; tidaklah baik meninggalkan mereka dalam keadaan yang mengharuskan mereka berkeliling meminta bantuan orang lain. Allah akan memberi pahala kepada orang yang membelanjakan apa pun dalam sedekah untuk-Nya.

Penyakit Sa'd bin Abi Waqas semakin hari semakin parah. Ia sangat mencintai Al-Madinah dan ingin meninggal di sana. Ketika Nabi melihat kegelisahan dan keinginannya untuk berada di kota kesayangannya, beliau meletakkan tangan mulia beliau di dadanya dan berdoa kepada Allah Yang Maha Kuasa tiga kali untuk menyembuhkannya.

Selama sakit yang sama ini, Nabi telah meramalkan bahwa Sa'd tidak akan meninggal sampai ia memberi manfaat kepada satu kaum dan merugikan kaum yang lain. Ramalan ini terbukti seratus persen benar. Kaum non-Arab sangat menderita olehnya sementara kaum Arab memperoleh banyak keuntungan. Setelah pulih dari sakitnya, ia menikah beberapa kali dan memiliki sekitar tiga puluh empat putra dan putri.

Sa'd bin Abi Waqas sering menangis deras karena takut kepada Allah. Setiap kali Nabi menyampaikan khutbah, matanya tanpa sadar dipenuhi air mata.

Suatu hari ketika Nabi sedang duduk bersama para sahabatnya, beliau memandang mereka dan bersabda bahwa mereka akan segera bergabung dengan seorang penghuni Jannah!

Seluruh sahabat terus menoleh ke sekeliling dengan penuh harap untuk melihat siapakah gerangan orang yang beruntung itu, yang tentangnya Nabi memberikan kabar gembira ini. Dan segera Sa'd bin Abi Waqas muncul, dan semua orang mulai memandangnya dengan penuh rasa iri dan terus menatapnya.

Inilah orang yang doanya dikabulkan, suci lidahnya, suci hati dan jiwanya, dan seorang pejuang besar. Dalam semua pertempuran yang ia ikuti bersama Nabi, di Badr, di Uhud dan di banyak pertempuran lainnya, ia telah membuktikan berkali-kali keberanian, keteguhan hati, dan sifat-sifat kepahlawanannya. Kisah-kisah heroik ini memenuhi lembaran-lembaran emas sejarah kita.

'Umar Farooq sangat terkesan dengan kekuatan iman dan keteguhan serta kejujurannya. Ia tidak pernah bisa melupakan peristiwa yang menggambarkan tingkat pengabdiannya kepada Islam. Ia telah memeluk Islam sebagai seorang pemuda; hal ini sangat mengganggu dan menyedihkan ibunya karena ia telah meninggalkan agama nenek moyang dan leluhurnya. Ibunya mencoba berbagai cara untuk membawanya kembali ke pangkuan kekufuran. Ketika tidak ada yang berhasil, ia menggunakan cara terakhir yang biasa dipakai ibu untuk menekan secara emosional putranya yang taat dan penyayang. Ini adalah sesuatu yang membuat tekad mereka goyah dan keputusan mereka gagal. Ibunya melakukan mogok makan dan menyatakan tidak akan makan sampai putranya kembali ke agama nenek moyangnya. Ia bersumpah lebih baik mati daripada melihat putranya menjadi seorang Muslim.

Sa'd bin Abi Waqas gelisah ketika melihat keras kepalanya ibunya, namun iman yang murni telah mengakar kuat di hatinya, sehingga kakinya yang teguh,

Seorang yang ditakdirkan masuk Jannah (Surga).

yang tertanam kokoh dalam Islam, tidak goyah. Ibunya hampir mati karena kelaparan dan kehausan. Menunjukkan tekad dan keberanian yang kuat, ia berkata kepadanya:

"Ibu tercinta, jika ibu memiliki seratus nyawa dalam tubuh ibu, dan setiap satu dari seratus nyawa itu meninggalkan tubuh ibu di depan mataku, meskipun demikian aku tidak akan murtad dan meninggalkan imanku kepada Islam. Terserah ibu apakah ingin makan atau tidak; adapun diriku, aku tidak akan meninggalkan Nabiku."

Melihat bahwa semua itu sia-sia dan putranya sekeras batu karang, ibunya mengakhiri mogok makannya. Keteguhan dan tekad Sa'd bin Abi Waqas ini telah diabadikan:

"Dan jika keduanya memaksamu untuk mempersekutukan dengan-Ku sesuatu yang tidak ada pengetahuanmu tentang itu, maka janganlah kamu mengikuti keduanya, dan pergaulilah keduanya di dunia dengan baik, dan ikutilah jalan orang yang kembali kepada-Ku dalam bertaubat dan ketaatan. Kemudian hanya kepada-Kulah kembalimu, maka Kuberitakan kepadamu apa yang telah kamu kerjakan." (QS. Luqman: 15)

"Ingatlah pentingnya Qadisiyah, yang merupakan tempat bersejarah. Jika pertempuran dimenangkan maka seluruh Iran akan berada di bawah kakimu. Qadisiyah adalah pintu utama menuju Iran. Jangan tenggelam dalam kesombongan karena engkau memiliki hubungan kekerabatan dengan Nabi. Jangan lupa bahwa kedudukan dan posisi seseorang di sisi Allah bergantung pada ketaatan dan kesetiaannya kepada-Nya. Allah Yang Maha Kuasa adalah Penolong dan Pemelihara segala sesuatu dan engkau adalah hamba-Nya. Jagalah

pandanganmu tetap terfokus pada misi Nabi, yang beliau wariskan kepada kita ketika beliau meninggalkan dunia ini."

Di akhir surat itu, ia memberitahunya untuk terus menginformasikan semua perkembangan terbaru. Bagaimana ia akan memasuki medan perang? Di mana ia akan berkemah? Seberapa jauh jarak musuh saat itu? Dengan kata lain, ia menginginkan informasi terperinci tentang apa yang terjadi setiap saat, seolah-olah ia bisa melihatnya sendiri.

Sa'd bin Abi Waqas, menunaikan kewajibannya, terus menginformasikan pemerintah pusat mengenai setiap detail hingga sedemikian rupa sehingga ia terus memberitahu Khalifah 'Umar Farooq secara terperinci dan menyeluruh tentang kesejahteraan dan aktivitas setiap Mujahid. Ia juga menulis kepadanya mengenai tugas-tugas individual dan kolektif yang diberikan kepada setiap Mujahid.
